title: Malicious user can manipulate reward calculations preventing other users from receiving correct rewards
description_excerpt: BribeRewarder reward manipulation allows attacker to skew reward distribution by exploiting deposit timing, preventing legitimate users from receiving proportional rewards.

title: User receives 0 rewards for periods participated in due to wrong _calculateRewards calculation
description_excerpt: Incorrect reward calculation in _calculateRewards returns 0 for users who participated in voting periods, causing complete loss of earned rewards.

title: BribeRewarder allows reward manipulation by malicious users
description_excerpt: Malicious users can manipulate BribeRewarder by exploiting deposit/withdraw timing to steal rewards intended for other participants.

title: Users cannot claim their bribe rewards due to incorrect claim logic
description_excerpt: BribeRewards claim function has incorrect looping logic preventing users from claiming earned rewards, causing permanent fund loss.

title: Missing remaining lock time check allows users double voting at end of locking period
description_excerpt: Voter contract fails to check remaining lock time for lsNFT positions, allowing users to vote multiple times by exploiting expiring lock positions.

title: Incorrect lockDuration used instead of remaining time in Voter.vote()
description_excerpt: BribeRewarder uses lockDuration (original lock time) instead of remaining lock time, allowing expired positions to participate in voting.

title: Improper msg.sender check in BribeRewarder._modify() function
description_excerpt: Wrong msg.sender validation in _modify allows unauthorized users to manipulate deposits and rewards in the BribeRewarder contract.

title: Pending rewards not processed when BribeRewarder.Deposit() is called
description_excerpt: Missing reward accrual before deposit causes incorrect reward distribution, as pending rewards are not settled before modifying user balances.

title: Funds can get stuck on BribeRewarder contract with no sweep mechanism
description_excerpt: BribeRewarder has no mechanism to recover stuck funds, and incorrect reward calculations can leave tokens permanently locked in the contract.

title: Incorrect check of ownerOf for tokenId during voting allows unauthorized voting
description_excerpt: Voting contract uses incorrect ownership check for tokenId, allowing non-owners to cast votes using others' staked positions.

title: Incorrect calculation of assets in getSupplyBalance() and getDebtBalance()
description_excerpt: PositionBalanceConfiguration.sol calculates supply and debt balances incorrectly, leading to wrong collateral/debt valuations system-wide.

title: executeLiquidationCall utilizes wrong debt and collateral balance disrupting liquidation
description_excerpt: Liquidation logic uses share amounts instead of asset amounts, causing incorrect liquidation amounts and potential bad debt accumulation.

title: Attacker may manipulate borrow interest rates of the protocol
description_excerpt: Interest rate manipulation through strategic borrowing/repaying allows attacker to artificially inflate rates, harming existing borrowers.

title: Incorrect deduction of accruedToTreasuryShares from totalSupply causing loss of shares
description_excerpt: Treasury share accounting error causes incorrect totalSupply tracking, leading to share dilution and fund loss for depositors.

title: _repayDebtTokens wrongly sets nextDebtShares leading to false liquidityRate and borrowRate
description_excerpt: Incorrect debt share update during repayment causes cascading errors in interest rate calculations affecting all protocol users.

title: Missing a conversion step from shares to assets in executeLiquidationCall
description_excerpt: Liquidation function fails to convert shares to assets, causing liquidators to seize incorrect collateral amounts.

title: Missing fee shares update to totalSupplies causes inconsistent state
description_excerpt: Fee shares not properly added to totalSupplies.supplyShares creates accounting inconsistency that compounds over time.

title: Incorrect accounting when executing mint to treasury in withdrawals
description_excerpt: Treasury minting during withdrawal uses stale values, causing incorrect share distribution and potential insolvency.

title: actualDebtToLiquidate incorrectly calculated by assigning share amount instead of underlying assets
description_excerpt: Liquidation debt calculation uses raw share counts rather than converted asset values, leading to under/over-liquidation.

title: Lender who deposits after interest rate increase cannot withdraw whole balance
description_excerpt: Interest rate accrual bug prevents late depositors from withdrawing full balance, effectively locking a portion of their funds.

title: User can sandwich their own position close to recoup all position fees
description_excerpt: Traders can front-run their own position closure with a mint, then back-run with a burn, recovering all accumulated fees from the position.

title: Fee precision loss disrupts liquidations and causes loss of funds
description_excerpt: Fee calculation precision errors in the PerpDEX cause liquidation transactions to revert or execute incorrectly, leaving bad debt.

title: Traders may decrease their trading loss via mint/burn
description_excerpt: Traders exploit mint/burn mechanics to artificially reduce their trading losses by diluting the fee pool before closing positions.

title: The api.burn function should have cool down period
description_excerpt: Missing cooldown on burn allows atomic mint-trade-burn exploits where users can manipulate LP share value within a single transaction.

title: User can seize more assets during liquidation by using type(uint).max
description_excerpt: Liquidators bypass collateral seizure limits by passing type(uint).max as repayment amount, replaying the repayment to seize excess collateral.

title: Attacker could update RedstoneOracle price to older one to liquidate positions
description_excerpt: Oracle price can be set backwards in time using stale Redstone data, enabling attackers to trigger unfair liquidations at manipulated prices.

title: Red Stone Oracle can time travel - atomic manipulation between different prices
description_excerpt: RedstoneCoreOracle can be atomically manipulated back and forth between different historical prices within the same block, enabling oracle arbitrage.

title: Liquidator can seize all collateral assets from all liquidatable positions
description_excerpt: Liquidator bypasses _validateSeizedAssetValue() check by exploiting calculation logic to seize 100% of position collateral regardless of debt size.

title: Exploiter can always bypass LIQUIDATION_DISCOUNT and seize all collateral
description_excerpt: Liquidation discount mechanism can be circumvented, allowing liquidators to take all collateral rather than the intended discounted portion.

title: No greater timestamps check in RedstoneCoreOracle
description_excerpt: Missing timestamp monotonicity check allows oracle updates with older data, enabling price manipulation through selective data submission.

title: Rounding error due to internal accounting allows stealing first depositors funds
description_excerpt: Share inflation attack through rounding errors in internal accounting lets early depositors steal from subsequent depositors.

title: Loss of users fund through share inflation attack in Pool.sol
description_excerpt: Classic ERC4626 vault inflation attack on Pool.sol where attacker manipulates price per share to steal depositor funds.

title: Missing slippage protection when redeeming rewards from LV
description_excerpt: Lack of slippage protection on LV reward redemption exposes users to sandwich attacks and MEV extraction during withdrawal.

title: Lack of slippage protection in VaultLib leads to MEV and user losses
description_excerpt: VaultLib operations execute without slippage bounds, allowing MEV bots to extract value from users through sandwich attacks.

title: FlashSwapRouter assumes 1:1 exchange rate between RA and CT+DS causing fund loss
description_excerpt: Router incorrectly assumes parity between RA and CT+DS tokens, leading to incorrect swap calculations and direct fund loss for users.

title: FlashSwapRouter mistakenly transfers RA tokens to DS buyers causing LV holder losses
description_excerpt: Router sends RA tokens meant for liquidity vault to DS buyers due to accounting error, directly reducing LV holder value.

title: PsmLib.repurchase() does not increase locked amount of RA
description_excerpt: Missing locked RA increment during repurchase allows withdrawal of RA that should be locked, leading to protocol insolvency.

title: PsmLib.lvRedeemRaWithCtDs() does not decrease locked RA amount
description_excerpt: Redemption function fails to reduce locked RA counter, causing accounting mismatch that allows double-spending of locked reserves.

title: Incorrect implementation of PsmLib.unsafeIssueToLv() function
description_excerpt: Unsafe issuance to LV has incorrect implementation causing tokens to be minted without proper backing, leading to insolvency risk.

title: DsFlashSwap.emptyReserve() incorrectly always returns 0
description_excerpt: Reserve emptying function always returns 0 instead of actual reserve amount, breaking reserve management and causing fund loss.

title: Incorrect psmRa in VaultLib._liquidatedLp() function
description_excerpt: Wrong RA amount passed to liquidated LP calculation causes incorrect distribution of funds during liquidation events.

title: VaultPoolLib::reserve() stores Pa incorrectly leaving funds untracked after expiry
description_excerpt: Incorrect storage of Protocol Asset in reserve function leaves funds untracked once the deposit expires, causing permanent fund loss.

title: Users redeeming with Ds in Psm will not decrease correct amount of locked Ra
description_excerpt: Ds redemption uses wrong locked RA calculation, allowing users to redeem more RA than intended, leading to insolvency.

title: Instant arbitrage opportunity in OracleMaker
description_excerpt: OracleMaker can be instantly arbitraged by exploiting price update timing, allowing risk-free profit extraction from the maker pool.

title: Two Pyth prices can be used in same transaction to attack LP pools
description_excerpt: Attacker uses two different Pyth oracle prices within a single transaction to exploit price discrepancy and drain LP pool funds.

title: LP can instantly arbitrage and drain any Maker by updating Pyth price
description_excerpt: Permissionless Pyth price updates allow LPs to atomically update price and trade in same tx, draining maker through arbitrage.

title: Funding fee rate calculated only on OracleMaker skew but applied across entire market
description_excerpt: Funding rate based solely on OracleMaker skew can be manipulated at low cost to generate extreme funding rates exploitable across all markets.

title: Whale LPs can make admin risk control parameters ineffective
description_excerpt: Large LPs can circumvent protocol risk parameters by exploiting position sizing mechanics, rendering admin safety controls useless.

title: Draining maker through funding fee manipulation
description_excerpt: Attacker generates extreme funding rate by manipulating OracleMaker skew, then collects outsized funding payments to drain the maker.

title: Attackers can lock market funds and steal funds in the maker
description_excerpt: Market fund locking attack prevents normal transaction execution while attacker extracts value from the maker contract.

title: Collateral tokens withdrawn by liquidateDefaultedLoanWithIncentive frozen in contract
description_excerpt: Liquidation function withdraws collateral but fails to transfer it to the liquidator, leaving tokens permanently locked in the contract.

title: _getCollateralTokensAmountEquivalentToPrincipalTokens uses incorrect min/max for oracle prices
description_excerpt: Oracle price bounds are inverted (min used where max should be), enabling sandwich attacks to borrow with minimal collateral.

title: Burning shares prior to computing withdraw value locks earnings forever
description_excerpt: Share burn before value calculation uses deflated totalSupply, causing withdrawal to return less than entitled and locking remainder.

title: Sandwich attack can take most of interest earned within LenderCommitmentGroup
description_excerpt: MEV bots can sandwich deposit/withdraw around interest accrual events to extract majority of earned interest from the lending pool.

title: Using slot0 to compute position price can be easily manipulated
description_excerpt: Uniswap V3 slot0 price used for collateral valuation is trivially manipulable via flash loans, enabling undercollateralized borrowing.

title: STANDARD_EXPANSION_FACTOR makes required collateral 1e18 times smaller than intended
description_excerpt: Collateral requirement divided by expansion factor instead of multiplied, making loans massively undercollateralized.

title: Liquidations in LenderCommitmentGroup do not include owed interest
description_excerpt: Liquidation calculation ignores accrued interest, allowing borrowers to default on interest payments without liquidation trigger.

title: Attacker can mint whole supply of Work tokens for price of one token
description_excerpt: mintBatch function charges fee only once regardless of quantity, allowing attacker to mint entire token supply at minimal cost.

title: Collection referrer share sent to wrong address - referrer gets collection referrer share
description_excerpt: Fee splitting logic sends collection referrer's portion to mint referrer due to variable naming error, causing permanent fee loss.

title: mintBatch uses msg.value in for loop allowing users to mint tokens for free
description_excerpt: Batch minting reuses msg.value in each loop iteration, allowing users to mint N tokens while only paying for one.

title: Calling claimDeposit after requestDeposit in same epoch leads to loss of deposits
description_excerpt: Claiming deposit in same epoch as request returns 0 shares due to unsettled epoch, permanently burning the deposit amount.

title: Claiming redemption in same epoch as request leads to loss of funds
description_excerpt: Immediate claim after redeem request in same epoch returns 0 assets while burning shares, causing permanent fund loss.

title: Exchange rate calculated incorrectly when vault is closed potentially leading to fund theft
description_excerpt: Incorrect exchange rate during vault closure allows attacker to extract more assets than their share entitles them to.

title: Malicious user can freeze all assets deposited in current epoch via claimAndRequestDeposit
description_excerpt: Griefing attack freezes all epoch deposits by exploiting claimAndRequestDeposit's interaction with unsettled state.

title: requestRedeem with sender != owner leads to shares locked in vault forever
description_excerpt: When msg.sender differs from owner in requestRedeem, shares are transferred to vault but redeem mapping set for wrong address.

title: ERC4626 Vault Inflation Attack on BaseLSTAdapter
description_excerpt: Classic vault inflation attack on LST adapter allows first depositor to manipulate share price and steal subsequent deposits.

title: Victims fund stolen due to rounding error and exchange rate manipulation
description_excerpt: Exchange rate manipulation combined with rounding errors in PT/YT system allows attacker to steal funds from other users.

title: YT holders cannot receive portion of principal allocated by PT holders due to manipulation
description_excerpt: Manipulation of yield token accounting prevents YT holders from claiming their rightful share of principal, causing fund loss.

title: All yield could be drained if users set any >0 allowance to others
description_excerpt: Non-zero allowance enables attacker to drain all accumulated yield from the Tranche contract through approval exploitation.

title: Attacker can steal unclaimedYield from any account in the Tranche
description_excerpt: Unclaimed yield theft via direct call to Tranche contract bypassing intended access controls, stealing accumulated yield.

title: Liquidity provider can grief LPs users rewards by siphoning fees
description_excerpt: LP exploits fee distribution mechanism to redirect fees intended for other providers to themselves.

title: Hacker can steal fee from LPs
description_excerpt: Fee theft from liquidity providers through manipulation of the trading fee distribution calculation.

title: Attacker can DOS raffle creation by canceling all PRIZE_LOCKED raffles
description_excerpt: Missing access control on cancelRaffle allows anyone to cancel raffles in PRIZE_LOCKED state, preventing all future raffle creation.

title: _lockedETH not decremented on raffle cancellation causing permanent fund lock
description_excerpt: Raffle cancellation refunds players but fails to decrement _lockedETH counter, permanently locking ETH in the contract.

title: Malicious user can prevent prize claiming for fulfilled raffles
description_excerpt: Attacker calls propagateRaffleWinner with invalid prizeManager/chainSelector, sending CCIP message to wrong destination and locking prize.

title: Malicious user can block prize distribution and raffle cancellation
description_excerpt: CCIP exception handling failure allows attacker to permanently block prize distribution by causing revert in message receipt.

title: No input validation for prizeManager and chainSelector in propagateRaffleWinner
description_excerpt: Missing validation on cross-chain parameters allows anyone to send winner notification to arbitrary chain/address, locking prizes.

title: Reward rate can be diluted in rewards contracts via zero-amount deposits
description_excerpt: Attacker calls depositReward with 0 amount repeatedly to extend periodFinish, diluting reward rate for legitimate stakers.

title: revokeVestingSchedule incorrectly decreases _totalSupply causing accounting errors
description_excerpt: Vesting revocation uses wrong amount to decrease totalSupply, breaking share accounting and potentially causing insolvency.

title: depositReward can be called by anyone with any asset leading to lost/locked funds
description_excerpt: Missing access control on depositReward allows anyone to deposit arbitrary tokens, diluting reward distribution and locking funds.

title: Attacker can nullify rewards in ZivoeRewards and ZivoeRewardsVesting
description_excerpt: Griefing attack through repeated zero-amount reward deposits extends reward period indefinitely, effectively nullifying rewards.

title: Users can still vote after their vest is revoked due to incorrect checkpoint update
description_excerpt: _writeCheckpoint not properly updated during revocation allows revoked vestees to retain voting power they should have lost.
